summary: Made the M2 video path evidence boundary explicit and machine-checkable. The validation bundle now records that the current sample path is grim materialized capture -> captured-sample.png -> captured-sample.rgb -> ffmpeg/NVENC process boundary with format=nv12, and that it does not prove direct capture DMA-BUF import into NVENC, zero-copy capture-to-encode, or no-readback capture-to-encode. Future direct DMA-BUF import proof is documented as future work, not completed evidence.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: The evidence boundary is explicit and machine-checkable.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: bundle-manifest.json includes evidenceBoundary.currentM2SamplePath and evidenceBoundary.machineCheckableClaims with directCaptureDmaBufImportIntoNvencProven=false, zeroCopyCaptureToEncodeProven=false, and noCpuReadbackAfterGrimCaptureProven=false.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: The current M2 sample path is documented as grim materialized capture -> raw/PNG diagnostic artifact -> ffmpeg/NVENC process boundary with format=nv12.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m2-video-path-validation/notes.md and evidence/m2-video-path-validation/bundle-manifest.json spell out the grim, captured-sample.png, captured-sample.rgb, ffmpeg/NVENC, and format=nv12 path.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: The evidence does not claim direct capture DMA-BUF import into NVENC, zero-copy, or no-readback proof.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: bundle-manifest.json explicitNonClaims and evidenceBoundary.notEvidenceFor list those non-claims; m2-nvenc-encode-sample notes clarify that NVENC evidence starts after diagnostic artifacts are materialized.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Future direct DMA-BUF proof is noted as future work and not done here.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: bundle-manifest.json evidenceBoundary.futureWorkForDirectDmaBufProof and evidence/m2-video-path-validation/notes.md state that direct DMA-BUF import proof is future work and not present in this bundle.

commandsRun.command: rg -n "direct capture DMA-BUF import into NVENC|zero-copy Linux capture-to-encode|format=nv12|captured-sample\.png|captured-sample\.rgb|future work" evidence/m2-video-path-validation/bundle-manifest.json evidence/m2-video-path-validation/notes.md evidence/m2-nvenc-encode-sample/notes.md evidence/m2-video-path-validation-does-not-prove-direct-dma-buf-import-in/report.md
commandsRun.evidence: Focused wording check found boundary and future-work strings in scoped files.
No file exceeded the repository line-count limit.

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node only clarifies evidence boundaries and non-claims. It does not implement or validate direct DMA-BUF import into NVENC.
